quotes.verbatim_quote:
the student would be wise to learn the chord-scale numbers
given below each pattern and use them for quick, easy transposition. For example, the 1-2-3-5
pattern of No. 44 could be applied to any new chord root, since numbers only indicate pitch relation-
ships, not the pitches themselves.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This establishes the method's core abstraction — scale-degree numbers as a transposition-ready shorthand — which underlies every pattern in the chapter.

quotes.topic: Major vs. minor triad bottom rule
quotes.verbatim_quote:
any of the patterns which follow, having the numbers (like 1-2-3-5) could be projected
over any tune’s entire length, when the minor forms of these patterns have been added to the fol-
lowing major forms, since nearly all chords contained in the tune’s progression will have either a
major triad on the bottom (i.e., 1-2-3-5) or a minor triad (i.e., 1-2-flat 3-5).
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the book's governing harmonic rule: virtually all chords reduce to major- or minor-triad bottom structures, so mastering these two pattern families covers nearly all real-world chord changes.

quotes.verbatim_quote: This pattern is made up of the first, second, third and fifth rones of the chord scale.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explicitly names the scale-degree content of the foundational ascending fragment, anchoring every transposition and application instruction that follows.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
This pattern is made up of the fifth, third, second and first tones of the chord scale.
Note that the direction is descending from starting note in each chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces the complementary descending fragment and its directional rule, completing the two-fragment vocabulary the chapter builds all subsequent exercises upon.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
This pattern may be

used on any chord with a major third.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Provides a concise harmonic scope rule for a specific pattern, telling the student the exact chord-quality condition that governs its application.

quotes.verbatim_quote: This pattern is used on minor chords descending in whole steps.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines a specialized harmonic context (minor chords in whole-step descent) for a pattern, equipping the student to recognize and deploy it in real progressions.
